message: Our family owns two acres of cultivable farmland in my father's name. Nobody in the family works for the government, nobody pays income tax, and my grandparents get no pension.
I'm a practicing chartered accountant registered with ICAI. My family owns three acres of farmland, no one is in government service, no one pays income tax and there are no pensioners at home.
We farm land that our family owns, but my mother works as a clerk in the state government.
I cultivate my uncle's field as a tenant farmer. My own family doesn't own any land.
My family owns farmland. My father is a retired government teacher whose pension is exactly 10,000 rupees a month. Nobody pays income tax and no one else works for the government.
I'm a 35-year-old woman. Our household is on the BPL list and we still cook on firewood — no gas connection at home.
I'm a 30-year-old man from a BPL family. We have no LPG connection and I want a gas connection in my name.
I'm a 40-year-old woman from an SECC-listed household. We already have an LPG cylinder connection in my husband's name.
I'm a 17-year-old girl from a BPL household and we don't have a gas connection.
I'm a 45-year-old woman and we have no LPG connection at home. My husband drives an auto and I do tailoring work.
I'm a Scheduled Caste student studying in class 12. My parents together earn about 2 lakh rupees a year.
I'm an SC student in the second year of my B.A. My family's income is about 3.5 lakh per year.
I'm an OBC student doing my B.Sc. My parents earn about 1 lakh rupees a year.
I'm a Scheduled Caste student in class 9. My parents earn about 1.5 lakh a year.
I'm an SC student doing a polytechnic diploma after class 10. My parents' income last financial year was exactly 2,50,000 rupees.
